outcome_text: Effectiveness of the employed TEVV metrics and processes are evaluated and documented.
scoring_text: Ability to assess TEVV process effectiveness for AI systems, including evaluating whether testing, evaluation, verification, and validation metrics are achieving their intended purpose, identifying measurement gaps, and documenting process effectiveness findings and recommendations.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to assess TEVV process effectiveness for AI systems, including evaluating whether testing, evaluation, verification, and validation metrics are achieving their intended purpose, identifying measurement gaps, and documenting process effectiveness findings and recommendations.
reference_document_element_description: Design and execute AI system test and evaluation programs, producing documented test plans, results, identified failure modes, and deployment recommendations.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.13 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-003 is the strongest match (STS: 0.675, cosine: 0.643). Secondary: AI-T-007 (AI, cosine: 0.635), RM-T-005 (RM, cosine: 0.624).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.675). Shared vocabulary: [ai, evaluation, recommendations]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, achieving, assess, documenting, effectiveness, evaluating] vs WIDAI terms [deployment, design, documented, execute, failure, identified] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.887 (P=0.874, R=0.901). R exceeds P by 0.028 — the WIDAI KSA's 20 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 33 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (94.3%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.643 ≈ STS 0.675 (gap 0.032). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, evaluation, recommendations] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, evaluation, recommendations]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, achieving, assess, documenting, effectiveness, evaluating]. WIDAI-only: [deployment, design, documented, execute, failure, identified].
